[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ikasuri|WD=Q10928586}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |image=File:坐摩神社.jpg |name= Zama Shrine |location=1-9 Hiranoya, Daitō City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|34.7075|N|135.63556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity={{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|lt=Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami|lt_ja=阿須波大神
波比岐大神|lt_de=Asuha Ōkami und Hahiki Ōkami|lt_zh=阿须波大神和波比岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |shrine_Rank=Village shrine |established=Unknown |main_shrine= |annual_festival=Third Sunday of October ||prefecture={{ill|Daitō|WD=Q386668}} }} '''Zama Shrine''' (坐摩神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Hiranoya, {{ill|Daitō, Osaka|en|Daitō, Osaka|WD=Q386668|lt=Daitō City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}.
[[Zama Shrine (Daito City)|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ It is related to [[Ikasuri Shrine]] == History == The exact date and origin of its founding are unknown. According to shrine traditions, Zama Shrine (Zama Daijingū) was the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja}} of {{ill|Nishinari District|de|Nishinari-gun|ja|西成郡|zh|西成郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinari District|1=Nishinari district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10968332}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}} in the 13th year of {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} ({{ill|1729|en|1729|WD=Q6952|lt=1729}}). The area where the shrine stands was originally a large pond called {{ill|Fukono Pond|de|Fukono-Teich|ja|深野池|zh|深野池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Pond|1=Fukono Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560330}}, which was reclaimed as "{{ill|Fukono Shinden|de|Fukono-Shinden|ja|深野新田|zh|深野新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Shinden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" through renovations of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} during the Edo period. Consequently, {{ill|Hiranoya Mataemon|de|Hiranoya Mataemon|ja|平野屋又右衛門|zh|平野屋又右卫门|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Mataemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, an Osaka {{ill|wealthy merchant|de|wohlhabender Kaufmann|ja|豪商|zh|富商|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wealthy merchant|1=gōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634379}}, who was granted reclamation rights by the {{ill|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|de|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|ja|真宗大谷派難波別院|zh|真宗大谷派难波别院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|1=Namba Betsuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583062}}, invited the spirit from {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Zama Shrine}} (currently in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}) as a guardian deity, marking the origin of this shrine. {{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} generally refers to five deities: Ikui, Sakui, Tsunagai, Hahiki, and Asuha. However, shrine records from the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō period}} specifically mention Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami as the two enshrined deities. Zama-no-Kami is revered as the protector of the {{ill|Tokyo Imperial Palace|en|Tokyo Imperial Palace|WD=Q500681|lt=Imperial Palace}} and worshipped for household protection, safe travel, and childbirth. West of Zama Shrine once stood the {{ill|Hiranoya Meeting Hall|de|Hiranoya-Treffpunkt|ja|平野屋会所|zh|平野屋会所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Meeting Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was demolished in 2008.[https://trystero.exblog.jp/7094448/ 大東市、平野屋新田会所が解体の危機 ] ==See Also== Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * revision.text:
[[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11425668]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to {{ill|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|en|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|WD=Q13429672|lt=Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion}} {{afc comment|1=to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Sazama Shrine (Daito City)]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ikasuri|WD=Q10928586}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |image=File:坐摩神社.jpg |name= Zama Shrine |location=1-9 Hiranoya, Daitō City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|34.7075|N|135.63556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity={{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|lt=Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami|lt_ja=阿須波大神
波比岐大神|lt_de=Asuha Ōkami und Hahiki Ōkami|lt_zh=阿须波大神和波比岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |shrine_Rank=Village shrine |established=Unknown |main_shrine= |annual_festival=Third Sunday of October ||prefecture={{ill|Daitō|WD=Q386668}} }} '''Zama Shrine''' (坐摩神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Hiranoya, {{ill|Daitō, Osaka|en|Daitō, Osaka|WD=Q386668|lt=Daitō City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}.
[[Zama Shrine (Daito City)|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ It is related to [[Ikasuri Shrine]] == History == The exact date and origin of its founding are unknown. According to shrine traditions, Zama Shrine (Zama Daijingū) was the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja}} of {{ill|Nishinari District|de|Nishinari-gun|ja|西成郡|zh|西成郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinari District|1=Nishinari district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10968332}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}} in the 13th year of {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} ({{ill|1729|en|1729|WD=Q6952|lt=1729}}). The area where the shrine stands was originally a large pond called {{ill|Fukono Pond|de|Fukono-Teich|ja|深野池|zh|深野池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Pond|1=Fukono Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560330}}, which was reclaimed as "{{ill|Fukono Shinden|de|Fukono-Shinden|ja|深野新田|zh|深野新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Shinden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" through renovations of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} during the Edo period. Consequently, {{ill|Hiranoya Mataemon|de|Hiranoya Mataemon|ja|平野屋又右衛門|zh|平野屋又右卫门|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Mataemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, an Osaka {{ill|wealthy merchant|de|wohlhabender Kaufmann|ja|豪商|zh|富商|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wealthy merchant|1=gōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634379}}, who was granted reclamation rights by the {{ill|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|de|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|ja|真宗大谷派難波別院|zh|真宗大谷派难波别院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|1=Namba Betsuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583062}}, invited the spirit from {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Zama Shrine}} (currently in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}) as a guardian deity, marking the origin of this shrine. {{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} generally refers to five deities: Ikui, Sakui, Tsunagai, Hahiki, and Asuha. However, shrine records from the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō period}} specifically mention Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami as the two enshrined deities. Zama-no-Kami is revered as the protector of the {{ill|Tokyo Imperial Palace|en|Tokyo Imperial Palace|WD=Q500681|lt=Imperial Palace}} and worshipped for household protection, safe travel, and childbirth. West of Zama Shrine once stood the {{ill|Hiranoya Meeting Hall|de|Hiranoya-Treffpunkt|ja|平野屋会所|zh|平野屋会所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Meeting Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was demolished in 2008.[https://trystero.exblog.jp/7094448/ 大東市、平野屋新田会所が解体の危機 ] ==See Also== Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|WD=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|WD=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine}} == See Also == *{{ill|Shinden development|de|Neulanderschließung|ja|新田開発|zh|新田开发|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinden development|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新田|1=shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503674}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == *{{Wayback|url=http://daitouburari.web.fc2.com/jinjya/zama.html |title=Zama Daijingū Hiranoya |date=20150430121347}} revision.text:
[[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ikasuri|WD=Q10928586}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |image=File:坐摩神社.jpg |name= Zama Shrine |location=1-9 Hiranoya, Daitō City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|34.7075|N|135.63556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity={{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|lt=Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami|lt_ja=阿須波大神
波比岐大神|lt_de=Asuha Ōkami und Hahiki Ōkami|lt_zh=阿须波大神和波比岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |shrine_Rank=Village shrine |established=Unknown |main_shrine= |annual_festival=Third Sunday of October ||prefecture={{ill|Daitō|WD=Q386668}} }} '''Zama Shrine''' (坐摩神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Hiranoya, {{ill|Daitō, Osaka|en|Daitō, Osaka|WD=Q386668|lt=Daitō City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}.
[[Zama Shrine (Daito City)|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ It is related to [[Ikasuri Shrine]] == History == The exact date and origin of its founding are unknown. According to shrine traditions, Zama Shrine (Zama Daijingū) was the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja}} of {{ill|Nishinari District|de|Nishinari-gun|ja|西成郡|zh|西成郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinari District|1=Nishinari district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10968332}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}} in the 13th year of {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} ({{ill|1729|en|1729|WD=Q6952|lt=1729}}). The area where the shrine stands was originally a large pond called {{ill|Fukono Pond|de|Fukono-Teich|ja|深野池|zh|深野池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Pond|1=Fukono Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560330}}, which was reclaimed as "{{ill|Fukono Shinden|de|Fukono-Shinden|ja|深野新田|zh|深野新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Shinden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" through renovations of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} during the Edo period. Consequently, {{ill|Hiranoya Mataemon|de|Hiranoya Mataemon|ja|平野屋又右衛門|zh|平野屋又右卫门|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Mataemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, an Osaka {{ill|wealthy merchant|de|wohlhabender Kaufmann|ja|豪商|zh|富商|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wealthy merchant|1=gōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634379}}, who was granted reclamation rights by the {{ill|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|de|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|ja|真宗大谷派難波別院|zh|真宗大谷派难波别院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|1=Namba Betsuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583062}}, invited the spirit from {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Zama Shrine}} (currently in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}) as a guardian deity, marking the origin of this shrine. {{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} generally refers to five deities: Ikui, Sakui, Tsunagai, Hahiki, and Asuha. However, shrine records from the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō period}} specifically mention Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami as the two enshrined deities. Zama-no-Kami is revered as the protector of the {{ill|Tokyo Imperial Palace|en|Tokyo Imperial Palace|WD=Q500681|lt=Imperial Palace}} and worshipped for household protection, safe travel, and childbirth. West of Zama Shrine once stood the {{ill|Hiranoya Meeting Hall|de|Hiranoya-Treffpunkt|ja|平野屋会所|zh|平野屋会所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Meeting Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was demolished in 2008.[https://trystero.exblog.jp/7094448/ 大東市、平野屋新田会所が解体の危機 ] ==See Also== Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|WD=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|WD=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine}} == See Also == *{{ill|Shinden development|de|Neulanderschließung|ja|新田開発|zh|新田开发|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinden development|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新田|1=shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503674}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == *{{Wayback|url=http://daitouburari.web.fc2.com/jinjya/zama.html |title=Zama Daijingū Hiranoya |date=20150430121347}} |date=20070105093652}} {{Shinto-stub}} * ==References== {{reflist}} }} {{shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|坐摩神社 [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ikasuri|WD=Q10928586|1=Ikasuri no Kami}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |image=File:坐摩神社.jpg |name= Zama Shrine |location=1-9 Hiranoya, Daitō City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|34.7075|N|135.63556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity={{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|lt=Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami|lt_ja=阿須波大神
波比岐大神|lt_de=Asuha Ōkami und Hahiki Ōkami|lt_zh=阿须波大神和波比岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |shrine_Rank=Village shrine |established=Unknown |main_shrine= |annual_festival=Third Sunday of October ||prefecture={{ill|Daitō|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}} }} '''Zama Shrine''' (坐摩神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hiranoya, {{ill|Daitō, Osaka|en|Daitō, Osaka|WD=Q386668|lt=Daitō City|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}.
[[Zama Shrine (Daito City)|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ It is related to [[Ikasuri Shrine]] == History == The exact date and origin of its founding are unknown. According to shrine traditions, Zama Shrine (Zama Daijingū) was the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Nishinari District|de|Nishinari-gun|ja|西成郡|zh|西成郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinari District|1=Nishinari district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10968332}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}} in the 13th year of {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} ({{ill|1729|en|1729|WD=Q6952|lt=1729|1=1729}}). The area where the shrine stands was originally a large pond called {{ill|Fukono Pond|de|Fukono-Teich|ja|深野池|zh|深野池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Pond|1=Q11560330|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560330}}, which was reclaimed as "{{ill|Fukono Shinden|de|Fukono-Shinden|ja|深野新田|zh|深野新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Shinden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" through renovations of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} during the Edo period. Consequently, {{ill|Hiranoya Mataemon|de|Hiranoya Mataemon|ja|平野屋又右衛門|zh|平野屋又右卫门|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Mataemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, an Osaka {{ill|wealthy merchant|de|wohlhabender Kaufmann|ja|豪商|zh|富商|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wealthy merchant|1=gōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634379}}, who was granted reclamation rights by the {{ill|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|de|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|ja|真宗大谷派難波別院|zh|真宗大谷派难波别院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|1=Namba Betsu-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583062}}, invited the spirit from {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Zama Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} (currently in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}) as a guardian deity, marking the origin of this shrine. {{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} generally refers to five deities: Ikui, Sakui, Tsunagai, Hahiki, and Asuha. However, shrine records from the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō period|1=Taishō era}} specifically mention Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami as the two enshrined deities. Zama-no-Kami is revered as the protector of the {{ill|Tokyo Imperial Palace|en|Tokyo Imperial Palace|WD=Q500681|lt=Imperial Palace|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}} and worshipped for household protection, safe travel, and childbirth. West of Zama Shrine once stood the {{ill|Hiranoya Meeting Hall|de|Hiranoya-Treffpunkt|ja|平野屋会所|zh|平野屋会所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Meeting Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was demolished in 2008.[https://trystero.exblog.jp/7094448/ 大東市、平野屋新田会所が解体の危機 ] ==See Also== Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|WD=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|WD=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine|1=Washinomiya Shrine}} == See Also == *{{ill|Shinden development|de|Neulanderschließung|ja|新田開発|zh|新田开发|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinden development|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新田|1=shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503674}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == Daitō City] *{{Wayback|url=http://www2.ocn.ne.jp/~webpeco/burari-24.html |title=Naniwa Exploration |date=20070105093652}} {{Shinto-stub}} * ==References== {{reflist}} }} {{shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ikasuri|WD=Q10928586|1=Ikasuri no Kami}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |image=File:坐摩神社.jpg |name= Zama Shrine |location=1-9 Hiranoya, Daitō City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|34.7075|N|135.63556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity={{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|lt=Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami|lt_ja=阿須波大神
波比岐大神|lt_de=Asuha Ōkami und Hahiki Ōkami|lt_zh=阿须波大神和波比岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |shrine_Rank=Village shrine |established=Unknown |main_shrine= |annual_festival=Third Sunday of October ||prefecture={{ill|Daitō|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}} }} '''Zama Shrine''' (坐摩神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hiranoya, {{ill|Daitō, Osaka|en|Daitō, Osaka|WD=Q386668|lt=Daitō City|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}.
[[Zama Shrine (Daito City)|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ It is related to [[Ikasuri Shrine]] == History == The exact date and origin of its founding are unknown. According to shrine traditions, Zama Shrine (Zama Daijingū) was the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Nishinari District|de|Nishinari-gun|ja|西成郡|zh|西成郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinari District|1=Nishinari district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10968332}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}} in the 13th year of {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} ({{ill|1729|en|1729|WD=Q6952|lt=1729|1=1729}}). The area where the shrine stands was originally a large pond called {{ill|Fukono Pond|de|Fukono-Teich|ja|深野池|zh|深野池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Pond|1=Q11560330|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560330}}, which was reclaimed as "{{ill|Fukono Shinden|de|Fukono-Shinden|ja|深野新田|zh|深野新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Shinden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" through renovations of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} during the Edo period. Consequently, {{ill|Hiranoya Mataemon|de|Hiranoya Mataemon|ja|平野屋又右衛門|zh|平野屋又右卫门|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Mataemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, an Osaka {{ill|wealthy merchant|de|wohlhabender Kaufmann|ja|豪商|zh|富商|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wealthy merchant|1=gōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634379}}, who was granted reclamation rights by the {{ill|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|de|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|ja|真宗大谷派難波別院|zh|真宗大谷派难波别院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|1=Namba Betsu-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583062}}, invited the spirit from {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Zama Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} (currently in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}) as a guardian deity, marking the origin of this shrine. {{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} generally refers to five deities: Ikui, Sakui, Tsunagai, Hahiki, and Asuha. However, shrine records from the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō period|1=Taishō era}} specifically mention Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami as the two enshrined deities. Zama-no-Kami is revered as the protector of the {{ill|Tokyo Imperial Palace|en|Tokyo Imperial Palace|WD=Q500681|lt=Imperial Palace|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}} and worshipped for household protection, safe travel, and childbirth. West of Zama Shrine once stood the {{ill|Hiranoya Meeting Hall|de|Hiranoya-Treffpunkt|ja|平野屋会所|zh|平野屋会所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Meeting Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was demolished in 2008.[https://trystero.exblog.jp/7094448/ 大東市、平野屋新田会所が解体の危機 ] ==See Also== Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|WD=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|WD=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine|1=Washinomiya Shrine}} == See Also == *{{ill|Shinden development|de|Neulanderschließung|ja|新田開発|zh|新田开发|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinden development|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新田|1=shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503674}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == Daitō City] *{{Wayback|url=http://www2.ocn.ne.jp/~webpeco/burari-24.html |title=Naniwa Exploration |date=20070105093652}} {{Shinto-stub}} * ==References== {{reflist}} }} {{shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ikasuri|WD=Q10928586|1=Ikasuri no Kami}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |image=File:坐摩神社.jpg |name= Zama Shrine |location=1-9 Hiranoya, Daitō City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|34.7075|N|135.63556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity={{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|lt=Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami|lt_ja=阿須波大神
波比岐大神|lt_de=Asuha Ōkami und Hahiki Ōkami|lt_zh=阿须波大神和波比岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |shrine_Rank=Village shrine |established=Unknown |main_shrine= |annual_festival=Third Sunday of October ||prefecture={{ill|Daitō|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}} }} '''Zama Shrine''' (坐摩神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hiranoya, {{ill|Daitō, Osaka|en|Daitō, Osaka|WD=Q386668|lt=Daitō City|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}.
[[Zama Shrine (Daito City)|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ It is related to [[Ikasuri Shrine]] == History == The exact date and origin of its founding are unknown. According to shrine traditions, Zama Shrine (Zama Daijingū) was the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Nishinari District|de|Nishinari-gun|ja|西成郡|zh|西成郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinari District|1=Nishinari district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10968332}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}} in the 13th year of {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} ({{ill|1729|en|1729|WD=Q6952|lt=1729|1=1729}}). The area where the shrine stands was originally a large pond called {{ill|Fukono Pond|de|Fukono-Teich|ja|深野池|zh|深野池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Pond|1=Q11560330|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560330}}, which was reclaimed as "{{ill|Fukono Shinden|de|Fukono-Shinden|ja|深野新田|zh|深野新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Shinden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" through renovations of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} during the Edo period. Consequently, {{ill|Hiranoya Mataemon|de|Hiranoya Mataemon|ja|平野屋又右衛門|zh|平野屋又右卫门|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Mataemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, an Osaka {{ill|wealthy merchant|de|wohlhabender Kaufmann|ja|豪商|zh|富商|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wealthy merchant|1=gōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634379}}, who was granted reclamation rights by the {{ill|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|de|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|ja|真宗大谷派難波別院|zh|真宗大谷派难波别院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|1=Namba Betsu-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583062}}, invited the spirit from {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Zama Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} (currently in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}) as a guardian deity, marking the origin of this shrine. {{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} generally refers to five deities: Ikui, Sakui, Tsunagai, Hahiki, and Asuha. However, shrine records from the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō period|1=Taishō era}} specifically mention Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami as the two enshrined deities. Zama-no-Kami is revered as the protector of the {{ill|Tokyo Imperial Palace|en|Tokyo Imperial Palace|WD=Q500681|lt=Imperial Palace|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}} and worshipped for household protection, safe travel, and childbirth. West of Zama Shrine once stood the {{ill|Hiranoya Meeting Hall|de|Hiranoya-Treffpunkt|ja|平野屋会所|zh|平野屋会所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Meeting Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was demolished in 2008.[https://trystero.exblog.jp/7094448/ 大東市、平野屋新田会所が解体の危機 ] ==See Also== Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Zama Shrine (Daito City)|lt=Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|WD=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|WD=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine|1=Washinomiya Shrine}} == See Also == *{{ill|Shinden development|de|Neulanderschließung|ja|新田開発|zh|新田开发|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinden development|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新田|1=shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503674}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == Daitō City] *{{Wayback|url=http://www2.ocn.ne.jp/~webpeco/burari-24.html |title=Naniwa Exploration |date=20070105093652}} {{Shinto-stub}} * ==References== {{reflist}} }} {{shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] [[ja:坐摩神社 (大東市)]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ikasuri|WD=Q10928586|1=Ikasuri no Kami}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |image=File:坐摩神社.jpg |name= Zama Shrine |location=1-9 Hiranoya, Daitō City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|34.7075|N|135.63556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity={{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|lt=Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami|lt_ja=阿須波大神
波比岐大神|lt_de=Asuha Ōkami und Hahiki Ōkami|lt_zh=阿须波大神和波比岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |shrine_Rank=Village shrine |established=Unknown |main_shrine= |annual_festival=Third Sunday of October ||prefecture={{ill|Daitō|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}} }} '''Zama Shrine''' (坐摩神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hiranoya, {{ill|Daitō, Osaka|en|Daitō, Osaka|WD=Q386668|lt=Daitō City|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}.
[[Zama Shrine (Daito City)|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ It is related to [[Ikasuri Shrine]] == History == The exact date and origin of its founding are unknown. According to shrine traditions, Zama Shrine (Zama Daijingū) was the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Nishinari District|de|Nishinari-gun|ja|西成郡|zh|西成郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinari District|1=Nishinari district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10968332}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}} in the 13th year of {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} ({{ill|1729|en|1729|WD=Q6952|lt=1729|1=1729}}). The area where the shrine stands was originally a large pond called {{ill|Fukono Pond|de|Fukono-Teich|ja|深野池|zh|深野池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Pond|1=Q11560330|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560330}}, which was reclaimed as "{{ill|Fukono Shinden|de|Fukono-Shinden|ja|深野新田|zh|深野新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukono Shinden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" through renovations of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} during the Edo period. Consequently, {{ill|Hiranoya Mataemon|de|Hiranoya Mataemon|ja|平野屋又右衛門|zh|平野屋又右卫门|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Mataemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, an Osaka {{ill|wealthy merchant|de|wohlhabender Kaufmann|ja|豪商|zh|富商|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wealthy merchant|1=gōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634379}}, who was granted reclamation rights by the {{ill|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|de|Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|ja|真宗大谷派難波別院|zh|真宗大谷派难波别院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshū Ōtani-ha Namba Betsuin|1=Namba Betsu-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583062}}, invited the spirit from {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Zama Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} (currently in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}) as a guardian deity, marking the origin of this shrine. {{ill|Zama-no-Kami|de|Zama-no-Kami|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|zh|座摩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} generally refers to five deities: Ikui, Sakui, Tsunagai, Hahiki, and Asuha. However, shrine records from the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō period|1=Taishō era}} specifically mention Asuha Ōkami and Hahiki Ōkami as the two enshrined deities. Zama-no-Kami is revered as the protector of the {{ill|Tokyo Imperial Palace|en|Tokyo Imperial Palace|WD=Q500681|lt=Imperial Palace|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}} and worshipped for household protection, safe travel, and childbirth. West of Zama Shrine once stood the {{ill|Hiranoya Meeting Hall|de|Hiranoya-Treffpunkt|ja|平野屋会所|zh|平野屋会所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranoya Meeting Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was demolished in 2008.[https://trystero.exblog.jp/7094448/ 大東市、平野屋新田会所が解体の危機 ] ==See Also== Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto
